hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.svelte|*.css) echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"VERIFY this file against project rules: 1) fs-* classes must NEVER be inside @apply — always as Tailwind classes in HTML. 2) Typography: only fs-* classes for font sizes, never text-sm/text-lg/text-2xl etc. 3) Spacing: only BBC GEL units (1-16), never arbitrary values like p-[20px] or m-[2rem]. 4) Z-index: only z-* from @enodo/tailwindcss-foundation, never z-10/z-20/z-50 or raw z-index. 5) SEO links (.svelte): <a> must have descriptive text, never wrap block elements in <a>. 6) HTML-first (.svelte): use native HTML (<details>, <dialog>, popover) before JS. 7) CSS vs Tailwind: custom CSS classes only for elements repeated many times in the final DOM — one-off elements use Tailwind directly. Report ONLY actual violations found in the file you just edited."}}'; ;; *) ;; esac; }
jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; [ -n "$f" ] && npx prettier --write "$f" 2>/dev/null || true; }
jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.svelte|*.ts|*.js) npx eslint "$f" 2>&1 || true; ;; *) ;; esac; }
npx svelte-kit sync && npx svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json 2>&1 || true
